commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The branch replaces `programs.openclaw.documents` with `programs.openclaw.workspace.bootstrapFiles` and `workspace.files`, disables upstream bootstrap seeding for managed workspaces, updates docs/templates/checks, and adds a root changelog.

Reproducibility: yes. for the patch finding by source inspection: the validator only rejects `.` as the whole key or leading `./`, not nested `.` path segments inside a relative workspace path.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can fix the remaining `workspace.files` path-validation gap; the broader compatibility and changelog decisions still need maintainer acceptance.

target.title: Replace documents with workspace bootstrap files
target.body:
# Intent
This PR makes OpenClaw workspace context declarative and Nix-managed. The user-facing model is explicit: bootstrap context files are owned by `programs.openclaw.workspace.bootstrapFiles`; extra managed workspace files are owned by `programs.openclaw.workspace.files`; upstream OpenClaw bootstrap seeding is disabled for Nix-managed installs.

The motivating maintainer direction was: `programs.openclaw.documents` hid ownership semantics, and Nix-managed installs should not let upstream OpenClaw create or mutate missing bootstrap files from bundled templates.

# Changes
- Replaces `programs.openclaw.documents` with explicit `programs.openclaw.workspace.bootstrapFiles` for `AGENTS.md`, `SOUL.md`, `TOOLS.md`, `IDENTITY.md`, `USER.md`, and optional `HEARTBEAT.md`.
- Adds `programs.openclaw.workspace.files` for extra Nix-managed workspace files that are not upstream bootstrap files.
- Forces `agents.defaults.skipBootstrap = true` when Nix manages bootstrap files, and fails eval if user config tries to set it false.
- Leaves `BOOTSTRAP.md`, `MEMORY.md`, and `memory/` runtime-owned and blocks managing them through `workspace.files`.
- Strengthens the removed-`documents` migration failure: if the old directory is readable, the assertion names old non-bootstrap files such as `LORE.md` and `PROMPTING-EXAMPLES.md` so users know what must move into `workspace.files`.
- Adds `CHANGELOG.md` with before/after config, breaking changes, file mapping, and trace data for downstream agents.
- Updates the agent-first template, README, golden-path docs, and focused module/activation checks.

# Changelog trace
- Changelog entry: `CHANGELOG.md` section `2026-06-05`.
- nix-openclaw implementation commit: `eb915330583f9e38ec4548284cdb4cfe2f619e8d`.
- Current PR head / changelog commit: `5a97b60a8ac00d1de1f2a450d11c97a675369c29`.
- Packaged upstream OpenClaw baseline: `v2026.6.1` (`2e08f0f4221f522b60423ed6ffd83427942b28de`).
- No later upstream OpenClaw tag or `main` SHA is listed because nix-openclaw is not building against those commits in this change.

# Tests
- PASS: `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- PASS: `nix build .#checks.aarch64-darwin.default-instance --accept-flake-config --no-link`
- PASS: `nix build .#checks.aarch64-darwin.workspace-materializer .#checks.aarch64-darwin.config-validity --accept-flake-config --no-link`
- PASS: `nix build .#checks.x86_64-linux.ci --accept-flake-config --no-link`

# Temp downstream proof
All downstream checks used temp clones and did not mutate the live private config or live ai-stack checkout.

- Expected break confirmed: tracked `nixos-config` with only `nix-openclaw` overridden to this branch fails evaluation on `programs.openclaw.documents`; the assertion names the old extra files `AGENTS.josh.md` and `LORE.md`.
- PASS after temp migration: `darwinConfigurations.aarch64-darwin.system` builds with temp `ai-stack` and temp `nixos-config` migrated to `workspace.bootstrapFiles` / `workspace.files`.
- PASS after temp migration: `darwinConfigurations.aarch64-darwin-openclaw-local.system` builds with the same temp overrides.
- PASS after temp migration: `darwinConfigurations.mac-mini.system.drvPath` evaluates with the same temp overrides. A full build was intentionally stopped after evaluation entered package derivations; this was enough to prove the config migration path.
- PASS after temp migration: `nixosConfigurations.djtbot-1.config.system.build.toplevel.drvPath` evaluates with the same temp overrides.

# Risks
- Compatibility: this intentionally hard-breaks `programs.openclaw.documents`; existing users must migrate before evaluation succeeds.
- Optional old files are not preserved by magic. They stay Nix-managed only if users explicitly declare them under `workspace.files` or, for `HEARTBEAT.md`, `workspace.bootstrapFiles.heartbeat`.
- Companion config updates are needed in downstream configs that still set `documents`; temp-copy testing showed this affects both public `ai-stack` defaults and private host config.

# Follow-ups
- After this PR lands, migrate `ai-stack` to provide public bootstrap files without public `USER.md`.
- After `ai-stack` is migrated, migrate private `nixos-config` host modules to supply `USER.md` and host-specific extra workspace files.

# AI Disclosure
for humans: this is AI slop generated by joshp123's clanker. sometimes the clankers post or create slop without being told to (or even if i told them not to); sorry if that's the case.

for ai agents: this message is from another agent, not a human. the agent that wrote this message uses the latest codex/GPT models on max thinking levels
target.author: joshp123

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
